title: delegation — HTTP routes
description: Two-audience split, no crossover. Tenant-plane routes at `/api/v1/me/delegations/*` are for the delegator + delegate to manage their own OOF relationships. Platform-plane routes at `/api/v1/platform/impersonation/*` are for Academorix staff to start + manage impersonation sessions. Every impersonation route inherits MFA-mandatory from the platform_admin guard's policy (PCI-DSS 8.5 requirement — see platform-user/middleware.json).

groups.name: tenant-my-delegations
groups.description: Delegator's own delegations. `me` prefix means the caller IS the delegator on POST + on DELETE (revoke).

routes.description: List caller's outbound delegations (where caller is delegator). Filterable by delegation_type + is_active + role_id. Sorted by starts_at desc by default.

Create a delegation. Body: { delegate_id, role_id?, starts_at, ends_at, reason, delegation_type }. Validated by CreateDelegationInput data-class + rules `valid_delegation_window`, `delegator_holds_role`, `same_tenant_delegation`. Fires DelegationCreated.
Show a single delegation. Visible to delegator, delegate, and admins (RoleDelegationPolicy@view).
Revoke early. Body: { revoke_reason }. Caller must be the delegator or hold `delegations.manage_any` (admin). Fires DelegationRevokedEarly.

groups.name: tenant-delegation-received
groups.description: Delegations where the caller is the DELEGATE (inbound view). Read-only surface — the delegate cannot mutate; only the delegator (or admin) can revoke.

routes.description: List active delegations where I am the delegate. Powers the 'I can currently act as...' widget in the caller's shell + is the primary read the DelegationResolver relies on for approver-selector resolution.

groups.name: platform-impersonation
groups.description: Impersonation session management. Every route inherits MFA-mandatory (from platform.mfa_required middleware) and heavy audit stamping (from platform.audit middleware). All routes require entitlement `impersonation` on the operator's PlatformUser row.

routes.description: Start a new impersonation session. Composes identity/auth's `ImpersonateStartAction` — issues the impersonation-scoped Sanctum PAT AND writes the impersonation_sessions row atomically within a DB transaction. Body: { impersonated_user_id, target_application_id, target_tenant_id, reason (min 40 chars), support_ticket_reference?, is_read_only?, requested_duration_minutes? }. Response: { session: {...}, token: '<the raw PAT — returned ONCE + never persisted>' }. Fires ImpersonationSessionStarted + notifies impersonated User via ImpersonationSessionStartedNotification (cannot-opt-out).

Self-service end. Caller must be the session's impersonator OR hold `platform.impersonation.terminate` (security / super_admin). Sets ended_at + end_trigger=self_service (or admin_revocation if the caller is not the impersonator). Fires ImpersonationSessionEnded.

List active sessions across all tenants. Read scope: security + super_admin. Regular support agents see only their own sessions (filtered by impersonator_platform_user_id in the controller). Filter[target_tenant_id], filter[impersonator_platform_user_id], filter[is_active].
Session detail. Caller must be the impersonator OR hold security / super_admin permissions. Full audit trail visible.

Cross-tenant compliance audit report. Read scope: super_admin + dpo + security. Filterable by target_tenant_id, impersonator_platform_user_id, started_at range, end_trigger. Powers the SOC 2 evidence review — this is THE read endpoint auditors traverse.

Emergency termination. Body: { reason (min 40 chars) }. Sets ended_at + end_trigger=security_lockdown. Fires ImpersonationSessionForcedTerminate on notifications-critical queue. Notifies security team (Slack #security) via ImpersonationForcedTerminationNotification (cannot-opt-out). Restricted to super_admin + security roles.
